I am hoping to contribute to a team that values thoughtful work and steady progress, and your program stands out as a strong match. I also completed a brand audit and strategy project for Jaguar Motors at Butler, where my team refreshed their positioning for younger consumers. These experiences showed me that I am at my best when I am researching, organizing insights, and helping shape the direction of a brand. I enjoy the process of understanding what makes customers care and translating that into clear strategic ideas. For Summer 2026, I am hoping to join a company that is steady, collaborative, and committed to thoughtful brand building. I would love to contribute to a team where I can bring curiosity, strong follow through, and a calm, steady approach to every project. I would value a chance to work in an environment that combines creativity with structure. I look forward to the opportunity to learn from your team and contribute to the work you are doing. Thank you for considering my application. Sincerely, Ben Cleek

job_label: Eiteljorg Museum Marketing and Communications Internship
job_description: Salary Range:  per hour. The Eiteljorg Museum of American Indians and Western Art in downtown Indianapolis seeks a motivated college student or recent graduate to assist the Marketing and Communications Department in its efforts to promote and raise awareness of the museum’s events, exhibits and programs during summer 2026. Through this paid marketing internship, a student can learn practical, valuable workplace skills that will bolster any resume. The summer internship would last from the end of May or early June 2026 until early August 2026, typically 20 to 25 hours per week during business hours on weekdays, with required evening and weekend events. Major responsibilities include assisting the Marketing and Communications staff with tactical creative execution of plans, uploading digital images to the museum website, maintaining daily presence on Instagram Facebook TikTok LinkedIn and others, updating online tourism and community calendars, taking photographs and shooting and editing video, writing calendar items news releases e-newsletter items and blog items, distributing printed materials, attending internal and external meetings, and other duties as assigned. Qualifications include major in public relations, digital marketing, journalism, advertising, communications, tourism or related field, experience with social media and social media marketing, Microsoft Office and Adobe Suite, some photography and video production and editing, and some experience with website builders and design. Internship is primarily in-person in Indianapolis, though some work may be done remotely with supervisor approval.

job_label: Business Process Analyst Global Services & Support
job_description: The Business Process Analyst for Global Services & Support is responsible for analyzing designing and optimizing end-to-end service and support processes to translate service strategy into coordinated cross-functional execution. The role acts as a bridge between Global Services & Support Finance Manufacturing and IT, ensuring operational needs are converted into scalable repeatable and well-governed systems. Responsibilities include owning end-to-end process design documentation and governance; identifying inefficiencies and friction points across workflows; facilitating alignment when tradeoffs arise; ensuring processes support internal controls audit readiness and operational rigor; partnering with stakeholders; serving as translator between business users and IT; owning intake and prioritization for system enhancements; translating business problems into functional requirements; collaborating with BizOps Insights on metrics; overseeing UAT; driving structured change management; establishing process governance standards; and ensuring documentation controls and ownership are defined. Qualifications include bachelor’s degree in business operations or related field, experience owning or materially contributing to enterprise-scale process design and/or system implementations, experience in scaled audit-aware or regulated environments, strong working knowledge of CRMs ERPs MRPs and related systems, ability to work through ambiguity and drive alignment, and exceptional communication organization and attention to detail.

job_description: Process Improvement Analyst II – Process Excellence. Candidates based in Florida are highly preferred OR fully remote open to EST and CST candidates. Required 2-4 years process analyst business process management project management and/or related experience. The role evaluates redesigns and enhances business processes across functional areas, supports operational excellence by applying process improvement methodologies, conducting research and analysis, and partnering with cross-functional teams to deliver meaningful measurable improvements. Responsibilities include process evaluation and design, applying Lean Six Sigma ISO Agile or related frameworks, data and root cause analysis, leading or supporting multiple improvement initiatives, facilitating working sessions with stakeholders, change management support, documentation and workflow development, performance monitoring, stakeholder consultation, training and presentations, quality and compliance focus, risk awareness, process integration, and maintaining awareness of industry processes trends and regulatory practices. cover_letter: December 1, 2025 Charles River Associates Recruiting Team Re: Application for Economics Consulting Analyst/Intern Summer 2026 Dear Charles River Associates Recruiting Team, I am excited to apply for the 2026 Economics Consulting Analyst/Intern role. I am a junior at the University of Michigan studying Economics with a minor in Statistics. Through rigorous coursework and research driven projects, I have built a strong foundation in structured problem solving and quantitative analysis. I have earned University Honors and recognition as a James B. Angell Scholar, and I am eager to bring that same intellectual rigor to Charles River Associates’ collaborative consulting teams. This past summer at Aspen Academies, a private investment firm focused on acquiring and scaling early childcare companies, I worked on strategy and diligence for potential acquisitions. For one target set, the data we needed simply did not exist in any usable form. I designed a mystery shopping outreach process to collect operational and financial information directly from market participants, then organized and analyzed the results in Excel to inform valuation and fit. That experience reflects the kind of work I enjoy most: taking an ambiguous question, building a practical way to answer it, and communicating clear recommendations to senior stakeholders. I also bring client facing instincts and strong real time judgment from luxury retail roles at Alo and Brooks Brothers. At Alo, I supported the launch of a new store that generated over $30,000 in sales within its first three hours, adjusting sales strategy on the floor based on live customer patterns and performance metrics. Across both brands, I learned how to build rapport quickly, listen for what a customer actually needs, and deliver a tailored solution under pressure. Those are the same skills I want to apply with consulting clients. Outside of work, I serve as President of the University of Michigan Men’s Club Ice Hockey team, leading a 30 athlete organization and managing a $150,000 annual budget. Balancing team operations, recruiting, compliance, and high time commitment athletics with a full academic load has strengthened my ability to prioritize, lead through fast moving challenges, and keep groups aligned toward shared goals. Charles River Associates’ focus on practical, data informed strategy and execution is exactly the environment where I believe I would thrive. I would love to contribute my analytical approach, resourcefulness, and people first working style to your team this summer.
extra_context: Strong consulting-interest student with strategy, analytics, and financial modeling exposure.

job_description: Planned Parenthood Federation of America and Planned Parenthood Action Fund seek a dynamic and effective Policy Analyst. This job reports to the Director, Public Policy on the Public Policy team, in the Research & Policy department in the External Affairs division of the national office. Purpose: Serve as a subject matter expert for key public policies that impact access to sexual and reproductive health care, coverage, and rights, with a focus on health care finance. Portfolio areas may include federal funding for reproductive health services, private and public insurance programs, drug pricing policies, and other policy areas related to the provision of safety-net health care. Engagement: Develops and maintains expertise in and tracks emerging and complex issues related to health care finance, coverage, health care delivery, health system reform, and sexual and reproductive health and rights, including identifying industry trends and conducting detailed, specific analysis of federal and state regulations, guidance, and legislation. Develops and implements short and long term advocacy strategies to advance proactive policy in the portfolio area and to secure, protect, and expand access to sexual and reproductive health services and providers through an equity lens. Writes for a variety of audiences and produces memoranda, fact sheets, issue briefs, toolkits, and other materials that detail specific public policies and their impact on sexual and reproductive health care. Drafts and edits comments on behalf of Planned Parenthood organizations in response to federal and state regulations and subregulations. Delivery: Collaborates and consults with other national office teams, departments, and divisions to advance administrative advocacy around assigned portfolio areas. Partners with the legislative affairs team to analyze legislative proposals and support Congressional advocacy efforts. Provides technical assistance and facilitates the development of policy advocacy strategies for Planned Parenthood affiliates to aid in their efforts to improve access to health care at the state level. Builds and maintains relationships with coalition partners and government officials. Knowledge, Skills and Abilities: Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred. Knowledge of federal or state government processes in developing policy, particularly administrative and regulatory policy. Knowledge of key public policies that impact access to sexual and reproductive health care, coverage, and rights. Familiarity with federal health funding programs and health care finance required. Minimum of 3-5 years of policy-related work experience. Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills. Understanding of the health care delivery system and safety net provider networks preferred. Experience implementing federal or state advocacy strategies to influence policy is a plus. Excellent verbal, written, and presentation skills. Commitment to health care access and the Planned Parenthood mission.

job_description: GeistM is a global full-funnel performance marketing company. We are seeking a Growth Marketing Analyst to join our Strategic Growth Team. In this role, you will help develop new concepts and strategies to drive client campaign performance across core advertising networks. You’ll work on content ideation, campaign optimization, data analysis, testing, and reporting, all with the goal of improving performance and scaling client accounts. What You’ll Do: Research, source, and manage marketing assets across multiple client verticals. Analyze campaign performance and generate actionable insights that support optimization and scale. Assist in developing content and creative concepts based on performance data and emerging trends. Execute campaign optimizations, define KPIs, and support structured testing across platforms. Manage and optimize campaigns across channels such as Meta, TikTok, Pinterest, Snapchat, X, Reddit, Taboola, and more. Support A/B testing initiatives including hypothesis development, setup, and interpreting results. Monitor pacing, performance shifts, and delivery trends to identify opportunities for growth. Prepare reports and performance summaries that translate data into clear narratives and recommendations. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support creative development and strategic planning. What You Should Have: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Economics, Communications, or related field. 0–2 years of experience in digital marketing, growth marketing, or a related internship. Strong analytical skills with experience using Microsoft Excel; comfort working with data sets, pivot tables, and formulas. Familiarity with digital advertising concepts such as CTR, CVR, ROAS, CAC, and marketing funnels. Interest in testing frameworks and data-driven experimentation. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Ability to manage multiple priorities, stay organized, and thrive in a fast-paced environment. Curiosity about how creative assets and messaging impact performance. Eagerness to explore AI tools that support creative ideation and campaign analysis. Location: NYC, hybrid with 1 required in-office day per week.
